EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Deep tropical moisture pulled up from Tropical Storm Eta and a tropical wave moving across the area resulted in periods of enhanced rainfall activity across the USVI on 11/10. Between 1 and 3 inches of rain fell.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Persistent heavy rains lead to urban flooding along the downtown waterfront as well as ponding of water in poorly drained areas, making some roads and streets briefly impassable.

CZ_NAME: ST. THOMAS / ST. JOHN AND ADJACENT ISLANDS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Tropical Storm Laura affected the area on August 22nd. Sustained winds between 30 and 40 mph with higher gusts were reported. Between 1 and 3 inches of rain were reported in Saint Croix with less than one 1 inch across Saint Thomas and Saint John.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wind gust of 41 mph was reported in Buck Island in Saint Thomas.

CZ_NAME: ST. THOMAS / ST. JOHN AND ADJACENT ISLANDS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Tropical Storm Isaias affected the area on July 29th and July 30th. Rain bands associated with the storm affected the islands with wind gusts greater than 40-50 mph reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 55 mph wind gust reported in Buck Island in Saint Thomas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Tropical Storm Isaias affected the area on July 29th and July 30th. Rain bands associated with the storm affected the islands with wind gusts greater than 40-50 mph reported.
